title: Set resource requests and limits to match real usage
description: Requests drive scheduling and QoS; CPU limits throttle and memory limits OOMKill, so size them from observed usage, not guesses.
body: Use when defining or tuning a workload's container resources. Do not leave requests unset (the pod becomes BestEffort and is evicted first) and do not set a memory limit below the real working set (it gets OOMKilled). Actions: measure actual CPU/memory under load; set requests at the steady working set and limits with headroom; consider omitting CPU limits where throttling hurts latency-sensitive work. Validation signals include no OOMKills, stable scheduling, and a Guaranteed/Burstable QoS class that matches intent. Risk boundary: changing limits on a production workload can trigger evictions or OOM; verify against usage metrics and roll out gradually.
